assertion_results.text: Data extraction identifies the ecosystem as RPM based on the library name (openssl-libs) and component context, not Cargo (Step 1 Ecosystem detection — read ecosystems from Ecosystem Mappings config)
assertion_results.evidence: data-extraction.md states 'Ecosystem | RPM' in the parsed CVE data table and explicitly explains: 'Library name `openssl-libs` is a system RPM package (not a Cargo crate or npm package). Ecosystem Mappings for 2.2.x stream confirms RPM ecosystem with lock file `rpms.lock.yaml`.' The ecosystem is correctly identified as RPM, not Cargo.

assertion_results.text: Version impact table includes ALL versions from the 2.2.x stream supportability matrix (2.2.0 through 2.2.4) and uses rpms.lock.yaml data for version extraction (Step 2.3)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md contains a table with all five versions: 2.2.0 (3.0.7-25.el9_3, affected), 2.2.1 (3.0.7-27.el9_4, affected), 2.2.2 (retag of 2.2.1, affected), 2.2.3 (3.0.7-28.el9_4, not affected), 2.2.4 (3.0.7-28.el9_4, not affected). Data source is stated as 'rpms.lock.yaml at each pinned tag in the Konflux release repo rhtpa-release.0.4.z'.

assertion_results.text: Dependency chain context classifies the package origin as explicit install because openssl-libs is present in rpms.lock.yaml, not inherited from the base image (Step 2.3.5)
assertion_results.evidence: version-impact.md Dependency Chain section states: 'rpms.lock.yaml: present → explicit install' and 'Origin: explicit install (openssl-libs is specified in rpms.lock.yaml)'. The package is classified as explicit install, not base image inherited.

assertion_results.text: Remediation creates a single task for the Konflux release repo — not the two-task upstream backport + downstream propagation flow used for source dependency ecosystems (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation — system package path)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md states: 'Since the ecosystem is RPM (system package) and the package origin is explicit install (present in rpms.lock.yaml), a single remediation task is created for the Konflux release repo.' Only one jira.create_issue call is present. data-extraction.md also confirms: 'Remediation path: single task (Konflux release repo), not the two-task upstream+downstream flow.'

assertion_results.text: Remediation task description follows task-description-template.md format with Repository, Target Branch, Description, Implementation Notes, and Acceptance Criteria sections — Files to Modify is intentionally omitted per remediation-templates.md (Step 7 Remediation Task Creation)
assertion_results.evidence: remediation.md task description contains all five required sections: '## Repository' (rhtpa-release.0.4.z), '## Target Branch' (main), '## Description' (Remediate CVE-2026-40215...), '## Implementation Notes' (update package version, regenerate lock file), and '## Acceptance Criteria' (openssl-libs >= 3.0.7-28.el9_4, Konflux rebuild). No '## Files to Modify' section is present, which is correct per remediation-templates.md for system package remediation.
